close_reason: Wyoming-tester is a Wyoming protocol client that doesn't directly use STT/TTS services. The server handles all STT/TTS internally using mellona. No changes needed.
description:
Update the wyoming-tester (src/wyoming_tester/) to be consistent with the mellona migration. The wyoming-tester is a push-to-talk emulator that sends audio to the chatterbox Wyoming server and receives TTS audio back.

Files to review and modify:
- src/wyoming_tester/cli.py: Main CLI

Current state: wyoming-tester likely sends raw audio via Wyoming protocol to the server and receives TTS audio back. It probably does NOT call STT/TTS services directly (the server handles that).

Review:
1. Check if wyoming-tester calls any STT/TTS services directly
2. If not, verify it still works correctly against the updated chatterbox server

If wyoming-tester has any direct STT/TTS calls:
   Replace with mellona calls following the same pattern as other migrations.

Update CLI arguments:
- Remove any --whisper-* or --piper-* specific arguments
- Add --mellona-config if tester needs to configure STT/TTS

Also verify: after the chatterbox server is migrated, wyoming-tester correctly:
1. Sends audio file to Wyoming server
2. Receives transcription response
3. Receives TTS audio response

This issue depends on: chatterbox-f1p (STT migration), chatterbox-tey (TTS migration)
title: Update wyoming-tester to use mellona for STT/TTS calls

description:
Validate and confirm that all necessary components are in place for Chatterbox backends to implement the Wyoming protocol features required by Home Assistant. Establish comprehensive testing infrastructure to emulate Home Assistant interactions.

**Key Deliverables:**
- Wyoming protocol research documentation
- Home Assistant emulator implementation
- Test wave file corpus (10-15 samples)
- Integration test suite
- Protocol flow diagrams and documentation
- Validation reports confirming all components operational

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Wyoming protocol fully documented
- Home Assistant emulator functional
- All protocol endpoints validated
- Test infrastructure operational
- STT and TTS services confirmed working
- Integration tests passing

**Reference:** dev_notes/project_plans/2026-02-18_epic-3-4-wyoming-llm-project-plan.md
**Specification:** dev_notes/specs/spec-wyoming-protocol-llm-integration-20260218.md

This epic consists of 10 task beads (3.1 through 3.10) that will be created as child beads.
title: Epic 3: Wyoming Protocol Implementation and Validation

description:
Deep dive into Wyoming protocol to understand actual implementation requirements.

**Priority:** P0 (Critical - blocks all other tasks)
**Estimated Effort:** 8 hours

**Subtasks:**
- Read Wyoming protocol specification documentation
- Study Home Assistant voice satellite integration code
- Analyze existing Wyoming service implementations
- Document protocol handshake sequences
- Document PCM audio format requirements
- Document text message formats
- Identify protocol version compatibility requirements
- Map conversation flows for STT and TTS scenarios
- Create protocol flow diagrams

**Deliverables:**
- Wyoming protocol documentation (markdown in docs/)
- Protocol flow diagrams
- Message format specifications
- Integration patterns document

**Definition of Done:**
- Complete protocol documentation created
- All conversation flows mapped and validated
- Team has clear understanding of implementation requirements
- No ambiguities remain about protocol behavior
title: Task 3.1: Research Wyoming Protocol Specification

description:
Generate standardized test wave files for repeatable testing.

**Priority:** P1 (High - needed for testing)
**Depends On:** Task 3.1
**Estimated Effort:** 4 hours

**Subtasks:**
- Define test utterances covering various scenarios
- Generate 10-15 wave files using Piper TTS
- Document expected transcriptions for each file
- Store files in source tree (tests/corpus/)
- Create metadata file with transcription expectations
- Verify files are loadable and playable

**Technical Details:**
- Use Piper with appropriate voice model
- 16kHz sample rate (typical for Whisper)
- PCM format compatible with Wyoming protocol
- File naming convention: test_001_turn_on_lights.wav

**Deliverables:**
- 10-15 wave files in source tree
- Metadata file with expected transcriptions
- Documentation on corpus usage

**Definition of Done:**
- All wave files generated and stored
- Expected transcriptions documented
- Files verified playable and parseable
- Metadata file created

description:
Validate full round-trip conversation flows through Wyoming protocol.

**Priority:** P1 (High)
**Depends On:** Task 3.6, Task 3.7

**Subtasks:**

description:
Update VoiceAssistantAgent (src/chatterbox/agent.py) to read its Ollama LLM configuration from mellona's config system rather than directly from chatterbox's Settings. Keep LangChain as the agent orchestration framework; mellona just provides the config values.

Current implementation:
  self.llm = ChatOpenAI(
      base_url=ollama_base_url,  # from chatterbox Settings
      api_key='ollama',
      model=ollama_model,
      temperature=ollama_temperature,
  )

New implementation:
  from mellona import MellonaConfig
  # Load mellona config using chatterbox's configured mellona config path
  mellona_config = MellonaConfig(...)
  profile = mellona_config.get_profile('default')  # or chatterbox's configured profile
  
  self.llm = ChatOpenAI(
      base_url=profile.metadata.get('base_url', 'http://localhost:11434/v1'),
      api_key='ollama',
      model=profile.model,
      temperature=profile.temperature,
  )

Scope of change:
- src/chatterbox/agent.py: Read LLM config from mellona profile instead of Settings
- src/chatterbox/config/__init__.py: Add mellona_profile setting (which mellona LLM profile to use)
- Do NOT replace LangChain - keep ChatOpenAI, ConversationBufferWindowMemory, agent executor
- Update tests that mock LLM initialization

Benefits: Ollama URL, model, and temperature now configurable via mellona config system. Consistent config source across all LLM usage in the stack.

This issue depends on: chatterbox-e8y (mellona config setup)
title: Update VoiceAssistantAgent to use mellona config for Ollama

description:
Copy or reference the mellona STT/TTS integration documentation into the chatterbox project so that agents working within the chatterbox submodule context have full information about mellona's STT/TTS API without needing to read the mellona source.

Why: Claude Code agents typically work within a single submodule context. By having mellona docs available inside chatterbox, agents can implement mellona calls correctly without leaving the chatterbox directory.

Steps:
1. Copy (or symlink) relevant mellona docs into chatterbox:
   - mellona/docs/stt-tts-integration.md → chatterbox/docs/mellona-stt-tts-integration.md
   - mellona/docs/provider-reference.md (STT/TTS sections) → chatterbox/docs/mellona-provider-reference.md
   - mellona/examples/config.example.yaml (relevant sections) → chatterbox/docs/mellona-config-example.yaml

2. Create chatterbox/docs/mellona-migration-notes.md with:
   - Which mellona version is required
   - How mellona is installed in chatterbox's venv
   - The specific mellona config file chatterbox uses
   - The Python API chatterbox uses (transcribe, synthesize, get_provider)
   - Common patterns specific to chatterbox's needs (PCM bytes → WAV file → mellona → bytes)
   - Any gotchas discovered during migration

3. Update chatterbox/docs/README.md to reference the new mellona docs

4. Mention these docs in AGENTS.md or similar if relevant

This issue depends on: chatterbox-a4w (migration complete, so we know what to document)
title: Copy mellona STT/TTS docs into chatterbox project context

All acceptance criteria from the planning bead description have been met.

Deprecated chatterbox STT/TTS service code removed:

description:
After migrating all callers to mellona, remove the deprecated chatterbox-specific STT and TTS service implementations and their dependencies.

- src/chatterbox/services/tts.py: Remove PiperTTSService, _MockPiperVoice (or the file entirely)
- src/chatterbox/services/__init__.py: Update exports

Dependencies to remove from pyproject.toml:
- faster-whisper: no longer needed (mellona has FasterWhisper provider)
- piper-tts: no longer needed (mellona has Piper provider)
- scipy: check if still needed for anything else; remove if not

CLI arguments to remove from src/chatterbox/main.py:
- --whisper-model, --whisper-cache-dir, --whisper-device
- --piper-voice, --piper-cache-dir
- CHATTERBOX_STT_* and CHATTERBOX_TTS_* env vars from Settings
  (replace with mellona config / mellona_profile settings)

Config settings to remove from src/chatterbox/config/__init__.py:
- stt_model, stt_device, stt_language, whisper_cache_dir
- tts_voice, tts_sample_rate, piper_cache_dir

Tests to remove/update:

Verify nothing else imports from the removed modules before deleting.

title: Remove deprecated chatterbox STT/TTS service code

description:
Implement a stateful, agentic conversation loop that enables Chatterbox to process natural language queries, invoke tools as needed, and generate contextually appropriate responses. This epic builds on Epic 3's Wyoming protocol foundation.

**Depends On:** Epic 3 must be complete before Epic 4 begins.

**Key Deliverables:**
- State machine implementation (LangGraph-based or alternative)
- Weather tool reference implementation
- Tool calling framework
- LLM integration with system prompts and tool definitions
- Context management system (implemented or designed for future epic)
- Integration with Epic 3 Wyoming protocol endpoints
- End-to-end conversation testing
- Documentation of conversation flows and state transitions

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Home Assistant conversation flow clarified and documented
- Agentic loop implemented and tested
- State machine handles all conversation scenarios
- Weather tool operational and accurate
- Tool framework supports multiple tools
- LLM integration working reliably
- Context management designed/implemented or future epic scoped
- Wyoming protocol integration validated
- End-to-end conversation latency <5s for simple queries
- Error handling robust
- Documentation complete

**Reference:** dev_notes/project_plans/2026-02-18_epic-3-4-wyoming-llm-project-plan.md
**Specification:** dev_notes/specs/spec-wyoming-protocol-llm-integration-20260218.md

This epic consists of 15 task beads (4.1 through 4.15) that will be created as child beads.
title: Epic 4: LLM Integration with Tool Calling

description:
Clarify exactly how Home Assistant handles voice assistant conversations.

**Priority:** P0 (Critical - blocks architecture decisions)
**Depends On:** Epic 3 completion
**Estimated Effort:** 6 hours

**Subtasks:**
- Study Home Assistant voice assistant pipeline documentation
- Analyze whether HA expects separate STT/LLM/TTS endpoints
- Determine if HA has built-in LLM integration or expects external
- Test with actual Home Assistant instance if possible
- Document conversation flow patterns
- Make architectural decision on endpoint structure
- Update Epic 4 architecture based on findings

**Research Questions:**
- Does HA send text to a separate "assist" endpoint after STT?
- Does HA expect Wyoming services to handle full conversation?
- Where does LLM integration fit in HA's architecture?
- Can we provide a combined Wyoming endpoint?

**Deliverables:**
- Home Assistant conversation flow documentation
- Architectural decision document
- Updated Epic 4 architecture diagrams

**Definition of Done:**
- Conversation flow fully understood
- Endpoint architecture decided
- Team aligned on implementation approach
- Documentation complete
title: Task 4.1: Research and Document Home Assistant Conversation Flow

description:

title: Task 4.2: Select and Evaluate Agentic Framework

description:
Design the state machine for managing conversation flow.

**Priority:** P0 (Critical)
**Depends On:** Task 4.2
**Estimated Effort:** 8 hours

**Subtasks:**
- Define all conversation states
- Define state transitions and triggers
- Design input handling (text from STT)
- Design LLM invocation logic
- Design tool invocation decision logic
- Design tool execution handling
- Design response composition logic
- Design output handling (text to TTS)
- Create state diagrams
- Document error handling strategies

**Proposed States:**
- RECEIVE_INPUT: Accept text from STT
- LLM_INFERENCE: Send to LLM with tools and context
- TOOL_DECISION: Parse LLM response for tool requests
- TOOL_EXECUTION: Execute requested tools
- RESULT_AGGREGATION: Collect tool results
- RESPONSE_COMPOSITION: LLM generates final response
- OUTPUT: Send text to TTS

**Deliverables:**
- State machine design document
- State transition diagrams
- Error handling documentation
- Interface specifications

**Definition of Done:**
- All states and transitions defined
- Error handling designed
- State diagrams created
- Ready for implementation
title: Task 4.3: Design Agentic Loop State Machine

close_reason: Added mellona as explicit dependency with local path configuration and aiohttp as transitive dependency. Updated implementation-reference.md with mellona documentation. Verified imports work correctly.
description:
Add mellona as an explicit dependency in chatterbox's pyproject.toml. This is the foundational prerequisite for all mellona migration tasks.

Steps:
1. Add mellona to chatterbox/pyproject.toml dependencies:
   [project]
   dependencies = [
     ...existing deps...
     'mellona>=0.1.0',  # or the appropriate version specifier
   ]

2. If mellona is not yet on PyPI (it's a submodule), configure it as a local path dependency:
   [tool.uv.sources]  # or [project.optional-dependencies]
   mellona = { path = '../mellona', editable = true }
   OR in pip terms:
   pip install -e ../mellona

3. Update docs/implementation-reference.md to note mellona dependency

4. Verify mellona is importable from within chatterbox:
   python -c 'import mellona; print(mellona.__version__)'

5. Ensure mellona installs correctly in chatterbox's virtual environment

Note: mellona requires aiohttp (not declared in its pyproject.toml) -- also pip install aiohttp.

This is blocked by mellona having a working STT/TTS implementation (hentown mellona issues must be complete first).
title: Add mellona as a dependency of chatterbox

description:
Refactor the CLI entrypoint to rename the 'chatterbox-server' binary to 'chatterbox' (update pyproject.toml [project.scripts] entry). Restructure the CLI with subcommands:

- serve: runs chatterbox in foreground (current default behavior). Logs to stderr unless --log-file is given.
- start: starts chatterbox as a background daemon. Stores logs at ./logs/chatterbox.log (relative to cwd), overridable with --log-file. Stores pidfile at ./logs/chatterbox.pid (relative to cwd), overridable with --pid-file. Creates the logs/ directory if it does not exist.
- stop: reads the pidfile (default ./logs/chatterbox.pid, overridable with --pid-file) and sends SIGTERM to stop the background service.

All existing arguments (--mode, --rest, --rest-port, --whisper-model, etc.) should be available under 'serve' and 'start'. The 'stop' subcommand only needs --pid-file.
title: Rename chatterbox-server to chatterbox with serve/start/stop subcommands

description:
Create a chatterbox-specific mellona config file that defines the STT, TTS, and LLM providers chatterbox will use. This config serves as the bridge between chatterbox's settings system and mellona's provider system.

Steps:
1. Create config file at chatterbox's config location (e.g., src/chatterbox/mellona.yaml or read from chatterbox Settings):
   providers:
     faster_whisper:
       model: ${CHATTERBOX_STT_MODEL}   # default: base
       device: ${CHATTERBOX_STT_DEVICE}  # default: cpu
     piper:
       voice: ${CHATTERBOX_TTS_VOICE}    # default: en_US-lessac-medium
     ollama:
       base_url: ${CHATTERBOX_OLLAMA_BASE_URL}  # default: http://localhost:11434/v1

   stt_profiles:
     default:
       provider: faster_whisper

   tts_profiles:
     default:
       provider: piper

   profiles:
     default:
       provider: ollama
       model: ${CHATTERBOX_OLLAMA_MODEL}  # default: llama3.1:8b
       temperature: ${CHATTERBOX_OLLAMA_TEMPERATURE}  # default: 0.7

2. Update chatterbox Settings class to include a mellona_config_path setting
3. Determine loading strategy: should chatterbox pass config_chain to MellonaConfig, or use mellona's default discovery?
4. Document the config approach in dev_notes/

This issue depends on: chatterbox-cxg (mellona installed)
title: Create mellona config for chatterbox integration

description:
Replace chatterbox's direct faster-whisper usage (WhisperSTTService) with mellona's STT provider in the core server components. The STT interface should remain async; mellona handles the underlying provider.

Files to modify:
- src/chatterbox/services/stt.py: Replace WhisperSTTService implementation to use mellona's transcribe() instead of faster-whisper directly. Keep the same async interface so callers don't need to change.
  OR: Remove stt.py entirely and update all callers to use mellona directly.
- src/chatterbox/adapters/wyoming/server.py: Update STT call sites if WhisperSTTService is removed
- src/chatterbox/adapters/rest/api.py: Update /stt and /stt/file endpoints to use mellona
- src/chatterbox/main.py: Remove STT-specific CLI args (--whisper-model, --whisper-cache-dir) that are now handled by mellona config

Migration pattern:
  # OLD: direct faster-whisper usage
  service = WhisperSTTService(model='base', device='cpu')
  result = await service.transcribe(audio_bytes, sample_rate=16000)

  # NEW: mellona STT call
  from mellona import transcribe_audio  # or similar API
  stt_provider = await mellona.get_stt_provider(profile='default')
  result = await stt_provider.transcribe(STTRequest(audio_file_path=tmp_path))

Note: mellona's STT takes a file path, chatterbox works with raw PCM bytes. The migration must handle the conversion (write bytes to temp WAV file, pass path to mellona, clean up).

Remove: faster-whisper from pyproject.toml dependencies (after verifying no other usage)
Update: tests that mock WhisperSTTService

This issue depends on: chatterbox-e8y (mellona config setup)
title: Replace WhisperSTTService with mellona STT in chatterbox server

description:
Verify that the chatterbox server starts correctly after all mellona migration changes, with no import errors or configuration issues.

Test steps:
1. Install updated chatterbox with mellona dependency:
   pip install -e '.[dev]'
   (verify no import errors)

2. Start server in STT+TTS mode:
   chatterbox serve --mode combined --debug --verbose

3. Verify startup log shows:
   - mellona config loaded successfully
   - FasterWhisper model loading (via mellona)
   - Piper TTS model loading (via mellona)
   - No references to old WhisperSTTService or PiperTTSService

4. Start server in full mode (with LLM):
   chatterbox serve --mode full --debug --verbose
   Verify Ollama connection established (via mellona config)

5. Verify REST API health endpoint:
   curl http://localhost:8080/health
   Response should show:
   { 'status': 'healthy', 'stt': true, 'tts': true, 'agent': true }

6. Test STT via REST:
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/stt -F 'file=@tests/fixtures/audio/test.wav'
   Verify transcript returned

7. Test TTS via REST:
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/tts -d '{"text": "Hello world"}'
   Verify audio bytes returned

Use --mellona-config to point to test config file (not personal ~/.config/mellona/config.yaml).

This issue depends on: chatterbox-a4w (deprecated code removed)
title: Validate chatterbox server startup after mellona migration

description:
Run comprehensive ha-emulator tests against the migrated chatterbox server to verify the full voice assistant pipeline works correctly with mellona providing STT and TTS.

Prerequisites: chatterbox server running with mellona migration complete.

Test script: Run ha-emulator against the running server with multiple test inputs.

Test cases (use all WAV fixtures from tests/fixtures/audio/ or chatterbox's existing test corpus):

1. STT-only validation:
   ha-emulator run-stt test.wav
   Verify: transcript matches known text, latency < 3s

2. TTS-only validation:
   ha-emulator run-tts 'Hello, how can I help you today?'
   Verify: valid WAV audio returned, duration reasonable

3. Full round-trip (STT → LLM → TTS):
   ha-emulator run-stt-chat-tts question.wav
   Verify: 
   - Transcript non-empty and sensible
   - LLM response non-empty and relevant
   - TTS audio file is valid WAV with audible content
   - End-to-end latency measured and acceptable

4. Multiple inputs (at least 5 different test WAV files):
   for wav in tests/fixtures/audio/*.wav; do
     ha-emulator run-stt-chat-tts "$wav" --output "/tmp/response_$(basename $wav)"
   done
   Verify all pass without errors

5. Error resilience:
   - Test with invalid/corrupt WAV file
   - Test with very short audio (< 0.5s)
   - Test with silence
   Verify: clean error messages, no server crash

Document: per-test latency, any transcription accuracy issues, any TTS quality observations.

This issue depends on: chatterbox-mja (server validated)
title: Validate ha-emulator end-to-end testing after mellona migration

description:
Improve logging in the chatterbox server:

1. Non-verbose (always on): Log whenever a new Wyoming connection is made (client address, timestamp). This is important for diagnosing Home Assistant connectivity.

2. --verbose flag (available on both 'serve' and 'start' subcommands): Enables detailed logging for:
   - Wyoming protocol: log each inbound/outbound Wyoming message (type, size, direction)
   - STT/TTS services: log Whisper call details (audio duration, processing time, transcript result) and Piper call details (text input, synthesis time, output size)
   - Ollama/LLM interactions: log the prompt sent to the LLM and the response received (with model name and timing)
title: Add connection logging and --verbose mode

close_reason: All ha-emulator tests passing (14/16 STT, 16/16 TTS, 14/16 full). All 358 pytest tests passing. STT service now properly initialized in full mode with audio resampling support.
description:
Run the full end-to-end test workflow and fix any problems discovered:

1. Start server: 'chatterbox start', then 'tail logs/chatterbox.log' to verify clean startup.
2. Run all 4 ha-emulator subcommands:
   - ha-emulator stt (STT-only suite against a test corpus)
   - ha-emulator tts (TTS synthesis test)
   - ha-emulator full (full STT+TTS round-trip suite)
   - ha-emulator single-stt (single-file transcription test)
3. Fix any problems discovered during ha-emulator testing.
4. Run the full pytest suite (pytest -v, excluding integration tests that require live external services).
5. Fix any test failures.
6. 'chatterbox stop' to cleanly shut down the background server.
7. Commit all changes when both ha-emulator tests and pytests pass.
title: Test all ha-emulator features, fix issues, and commit

description:
Replace chatterbox's direct piper-tts usage (PiperTTSService) with mellona's TTS provider in the core server components.

Files to modify:
- src/chatterbox/services/tts.py: Replace PiperTTSService implementation with mellona TTS calls. Keep the same async interface (synthesize() returning bytes) so Wyoming and REST callers don't change.
  OR: Remove tts.py entirely and update all callers to use mellona directly.
- src/chatterbox/adapters/wyoming/server.py: Update TTS call sites (Synthesize event handler)
- src/chatterbox/adapters/rest/api.py: Update /tts endpoint
- src/chatterbox/tools/registry.py: Update TTSTool if it calls PiperTTSService directly
- src/chatterbox/main.py: Remove TTS-specific CLI args (--piper-voice, --piper-cache-dir) that are now mellona config

Migration pattern:
  # OLD: direct piper-tts usage
  service = PiperTTSService(voice='en_US-lessac-medium')
  audio_bytes = await service.synthesize(text)

  # NEW: mellona TTS call
  tts_provider = await mellona.get_tts_provider(profile='default')
  response = await tts_provider.synthesize(TTSRequest(text=text, output_format='wav'))
  audio_bytes = response.audio_data

Audio format note: Chatterbox's Wyoming server expects PCM audio at specific sample rate. Ensure mellona's Piper provider returns compatible format, OR convert after mellona call.

Remove: piper-tts from pyproject.toml dependencies (after verifying no other usage)
Remove: _MockPiperVoice class (no longer needed)
Update: tests that mock PiperTTSService

This issue depends on: chatterbox-e8y (mellona config setup)
title: Replace PiperTTSService with mellona TTS in chatterbox server

description:
Run wyoming-tester against the migrated chatterbox server to verify the Wyoming protocol pipeline works correctly.

Prerequisites: chatterbox server running with Wyoming protocol enabled.

Test cases:

1. Text-based test (no audio):
   chatterbox-wyoming-client 'What time is it?'
   Verify: response text received and reasonable

2. Audio push-to-talk test:
   wyoming-tester --uri tcp://localhost:10700 --file tests/fixtures/audio/greeting.wav
   Verify: transcription and TTS response received

3. Multiple audio files:
   for wav in tests/fixtures/audio/*.wav; do
     wyoming-tester --uri tcp://localhost:10700 --file "$wav"
   done
   Verify all complete without protocol errors

4. Test different server modes:
   - stt_only mode: send audio, receive transcript
   - tts_only mode: send text, receive audio
   - full mode: send audio, receive audio response

5. Latency validation:
   Measure round-trip time for each test
   Document baseline latency for future regression comparison

6. Protocol compatibility:
   Verify Wyoming protocol messages are correctly formed (no protocol errors in server logs)

Note: wyoming-tester emulates the ESP32-S3-BOX-3B hardware behavior. Correct operation here validates real hardware compatibility.

This issue depends on: chatterbox-mja (server validated)
title: Validate wyoming-tester end-to-end testing after mellona migration
